title: Classifying Social Media Posts
text: cat.vader thin domain wrapper around cat.stack adds social-media platform connectors plus prompt framing tuned short, informal, online language. can use two ways: Pull posts directly platform (Threads, Reddit, Bluesky, Mastodon, YouTube, etc.) using sm_source, sm_handle, sm_credentials arguments, classify. Classify text already plain character vector — identical cat.stack::classify() social-media-aware prompt context. Everything else — supported models, output format, ensemble voting — identical cat.stack.

title: Classifying Social Media Posts
Classifying Social Media Posts
text: simplest path — pass character vector post text:
code: posts <- c(   "Just had the best coffee ever! Highly recommend the new place downtown.",   "Politicians are all the same. Nothing ever changes.",   "Looking forward to the game tonight!",   "This new policy is going to ruin small businesses.",   "Anyone know a good vet in the area?" )  results <- classify(   input_data = posts,   categories = c("Positive sentiment", "Negative sentiment",                  "Question/request", "Other"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )

what: Pull and classify in one call
title: Classifying Social Media Posts
text: want analyse posting history public account, cat.vader can pull posts directly feed classifier: returned data.frame includes original text platform engagement metrics (likes, replies, reposts, etc.) can correlate content categories reach. platform authentication setup; check cat-vader Python docs current credential formats.
code: # Authenticate once with your Threads API credentials, then: results <- classify(   sm_source      = "threads",   sm_handle      = "your_username",   sm_months      = 6L,                      # last 6 months   sm_credentials = Sys.getenv("THREADS_TOKEN"),   categories     = c("Personal", "Political", "Promotional",                      "Question", "Other"),   api_key        = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model     = "gpt-4o-mini" )

title: Classifying Social Media Posts
text: classifying, see themes actually present: iterate — drop noise categories, merge similar ones, re-run classify() cleaned scheme.
code: cats <- extract(   input_data     = df$posts,   max_categories = 10L,   api_key        = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model     = "gpt-4o-mini" ) cats$top_categories

title: Classifying Social Media Posts
text: Short text harder. Tweets/posts often lack context ("lol", ""). Including platform name brief description description= helps model interpret short posts. Emoji slang. Frontier models handle well; older smaller models less . ’re seeing weird classifications highly informal text, try gpt-4o claude-3-5-sonnet mini/haiku tier. Engagement isn’t ground truth. high-engagement post isn’t necessarily correctly classified — engagement reflects audience, category. Validate hand-coded subsample.

title: Classifying Social Media Posts
text: Full Getting Started guide: vignette("getting-started", package = "cat.llm") Per-function reference: ?cat.vader::classify, ?cat.vader::extract, ?cat.vader::explore Platform-connector docs maintained Python catvader package.

what: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
title: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
text: Social media content classification LLMs. domain wrapper around cat.stack adds social media sourcing parameters classifying posts Reddit, Twitter/X, YouTube, platforms. cat.vader wraps Python catvader package via reticulate.

title: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended) install.packages("cat.vader",                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # Or from a local clone devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack") devtools::install("path/to/cat.vader")  # Install the Python backend (one-time setup) # pip install cat-vader

title: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
code: library(cat.vader)  # Fetch and classify Reddit posts results <- classify(   categories     = c("Political", "Economic", "Social", "Other"),   sm_source      = "reddit",   sm_handle      = "politics",   sm_limit       = 100L,   sm_months      = 3L,   api_key        = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   sm_credentials = list(     client_id     = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET")   ) )  # Classify pre-loaded data results <- classify(   input_data  = df$posts,   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   description = "Social media sentiment",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
code: result <- extract(   sm_source      = "reddit",   sm_handle      = "technology",   sm_limit       = 200L,   api_key        = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   sm_credentials = list(     client_id     = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_CLIENT_ID"),     client_secret = Sys.getenv("REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET")   ) ) print(result$top_categories)

title: cat.vader — Social media classification for R
text: MIT

what: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
title: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
text: Wraps Python catvader.classify() function. Adds social media sourcing parameters base cat.stack classification engine.

title: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
code: classify(   input_data = NULL,   categories = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   sm_source = NULL,   sm_limit = 50L,   sm_months = NULL,   sm_days = NULL,   sm_credentials = NULL,   sm_handle = NULL,   sm_timezone = "UTC",   sm_youtube_content = "video",   sm_youtube_transcript = FALSE,   sm_comments_per_video = 20L,   sm_youtube_transcript_max_chars = 10000L,   platform = NULL,   handle = NULL,   hashtags = NULL,   post_metadata = NULL,   description = "",   feed_question = "",   user_model = "gpt-4o",   mode = "image",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_verification = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   thinking_budget = 0L,   example1 = NULL,   example2 = NULL,   example3 = NULL,   example4 = NULL,   example5 = NULL,   example6 = NULL,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 10L,   research_question = NULL,   models = NULL,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   use_json_schema = TRUE,   max_workers = NULL,   fail_strategy = "partial",   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   pdf_dpi = 150L,   auto_download = FALSE,   add_other = "prompt",   check_verbosity = TRUE,   prompt_tune = NULL,   tune_iterations = 1L,   tune_ui = "browser",   tune_optimize = "balanced" )

title: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column, NULL fetch social media. Default NULL. categories character vector category names, "auto". Default NULL. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. sm_source Character NULL. Social media source (e.g., "reddit", "twitter", "youtube"). sm_limit Integer. Max posts fetch. Default 50L. sm_months Integer NULL. Fetch posts last N months. sm_days Integer NULL. Fetch posts last N days. sm_credentials Named list NULL. API credentials social media platform. sm_handle Character NULL. Social media handle fetch . sm_timezone Character. Timezone date filtering. Default "UTC". sm_youtube_content Character. YouTube content type. Default "video". sm_youtube_transcript Logical. Include YouTube transcripts. Default FALSE. sm_comments_per_video Integer. Comments per YouTube video. Default 20L. sm_youtube_transcript_max_chars Integer. Max transcript chars. Default 10000L. platform Character NULL. Alias sm_source. handle Character NULL. Alias sm_handle. hashtags Character vector NULL. Hashtags filter . post_metadata Named list NULL. Additional post metadata. description Character. Context description. Default "". feed_question Character. Feed-specific question context. Default "". user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". mode Character. Processing mode. Default "image". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. safety Logical. Save progress item. Default FALSE. chain_of_verification Logical. Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. example1, example2, example3, example4, example5, example6 Optional -shot examples. filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. save_directory Character NULL. Output directory. model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 10L. research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. models List model specs ensemble mode. consensus_threshold Character numeric. Default "unanimous". use_json_schema Logical. Default TRUE. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. add_other Logical "prompt". Default "prompt". check_verbosity Logical. Default TRUE. prompt_tune Integer NULL. Rows sampled per APO correction round. Default NULL. tune_iterations Integer. APO optimization passes. Default 1L. tune_ui Character. Correction UI: "browser" "terminal". Default "browser". tune_optimize Character. Metric optimize: "balanced", "sensitivity", "precision". Default "balanced".

title: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
text: data.frame classification results.

title: Classify social media content using LLMs — classify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Classify your own text directly results <- classify(   input_data = c("Just had the best coffee ever!",                  "Politicians are all the same",                  "Looking forward to the game tonight"),   categories = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" ) } # }

what: Explore raw categories in social media data — explore
title: Explore raw categories in social media data — explore
text: Wraps Python catvader.explore() function. Returns every category string extracted every chunk across every iteration – duplicates intact.

title: Explore raw categories in social media data — explore
code: explore(   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   sm_source = NULL,   sm_limit = 50L,   sm_months = NULL,   sm_credentials = NULL,   platform = NULL,   handle = NULL,   hashtags = NULL,   post_metadata = NULL,   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0 )

Default "". sm_source Character NULL. Social media source. sm_limit Integer. Max posts fetch. Default 50L. sm_months Integer NULL. Fetch posts last N months. sm_credentials Named list NULL. API credentials. platform Character NULL. Alias sm_source. handle Character NULL. Social media handle. hashtags Character vector NULL. Hashtags filter . post_metadata Named list NULL. Additional post metadata. max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 12L. user_model Character. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. focus Character NULL. chunk_delay Numeric. Default 0.0.

title: Explore raw categories in social media data — explore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ raw_cats <- explore(   input_data = df$posts,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini",   iterations = 4L ) table(raw_cats) } # }

title: cat.vader 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catvader package. Domain wrapper around cat.stack adapted social media data. Connects Threads API (social platform connectors) pull post archives, classify , return enriched datasets engagement metrics. Exports classify(), extract(), explore().
